page: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the n4a server family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 processor architecture, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, designed for workloads requiring a larger memory footprint relative to compute capacity.

This instance type does not include local NVMe SSD storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, the instance does not bundle any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. These hardware constraints make it a specialized option focused purely on memory-dense CPU execution rather than local storage speed or graphics acceleration.

From a resource density perspective, the n4a-highmem-4 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, providing qualitative cost efficiency for applications that are bound by memory capacity rather than raw processing power. Appropriate workloads for this profile include small-to-medium databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise applications with moderate CPU demands, and lightweight data processing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highmem-4 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the n4a instance family, configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory. This allocation provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, positioning the server for memory-demanding tasks. The instance does not feature local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring external network storage for data persistence. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile is designed for workloads that require substantial memory capacity relative to compute resources without the need for local disk performance or graphics processing. It is suitable for in-memory databases, caching systems, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
